name: The World (XXI)
shortDescription: The World represents completion, integration, and fulfillment. It signifies the end of a cycle and the beginning of a new one, embodying wholeness and cosmic consciousness.
description: The World, card number XXI in the Major Arcana of the Tarot, signifies completion, integration, and the culmination of a journey. It represents the end of one cycle and the imminent beginning of another, holding the promise of fulfillment and understanding. The card often depicts a female figure, sometimes nude or semi-nude, dancing or floating within a laurel wreath. This figure embodies the anima mundi, the soul of the world, representing wholeness and the interconnectedness of all things. The laurel wreath symbolizes victory, achievement, and the successful completion of a significant undertaking. In many depictions, the four corners of the card feature the symbols of the four evangelists (or fixed astrological signs): an angel (Aquarius/Matthew), an eagle (Scorpio/John), a bull (Taurus/Luke), and a lion (Leo/Mark). These figures represent the four elements (air, water, earth, fire) and the four corners of the universe, highlighting the card's theme of cosmic consciousness and universal harmony. The World card encourages embracing the lessons learned and integrating them into one's being to move forward with wisdom and clarity. It suggests that the individual has reached a state of balance and understanding, ready to embark on new adventures with a deeper sense of self and purpose. It is a card of profound optimism, signifying the potential for lasting success and the realization of one's full potential. The card's message is one of unity and interconnectedness, emphasizing the importance of recognizing our place within the larger cosmic order.

sources.title: The Pictorial Key to the Tarot
sources.author: A.E. Waite

sources.title: Meditations on the Tarot: A Journey into Christian Hermeticism
sources.author: Anonymous

sources.title: 78 Degrees Wisdom: A Book of Tarot
sources.author: Rachel Pollack

worshipPractices.name: The Dance of Completion
worshipPractices.description: Followers engage in ritualistic dances symbolizing the end of a cycle and the welcoming of a new beginning. These dances often incorporate elements of nature, celebrating the cyclical nature of life and the universe, aiming to embody the World's wholeness.

worshipPractices.name: The Meditation of Integration
worshipPractices.description: Practitioners meditate on the different aspects of their lives, seeking to integrate them into a unified whole. This involves acknowledging both positive and negative experiences and finding peace and acceptance with the journey, aiming for enlightenment through self-acceptance.

festivals.name: The Festival of Culmination
festivals.timing: The final day of the harvest season.
festivals.description: A celebration of the year's achievements and the lessons learned, marking the end of the agricultural cycle. Community members gather to share food, stories, and express gratitude for the bounty of the year, anticipating the seed of the new year.

festivals.name: The Day of Universal Harmony
festivals.description: A day dedicated to promoting peace and understanding between all people. Celebrations involve acts of kindness, reconciliation ceremonies, and efforts to bridge cultural divides, reflecting the World's encompassing nature.

cultural.modernLegacy: The World's symbolism is frequently used in modern art and literature to represent personal growth, achieving goals, and finding inner peace. The imagery of completion and interconnectedness is also prevalent in contemporary philosophy and spirituality, influencing movements promoting global unity and environmental consciousness.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Brahman represents the ultimate reality and the source of all existence, mirroring the World card's representation of completion and the interconnectedness of everything. It is the culmination of spiritual understanding and the potential for a new cycle of creation.

Olodumare is the supreme being, the source of all creation and the ultimate authority in the Yoruba pantheon, reflecting the World card's theme of encompassing all aspects of existence and representing the ultimate achievement.

Dreamtime is not a place but a state of being that contains the past, present, and future. It represents the totality of existence and the cyclical nature of life, mirroring the World card's depiction of completion leading to a new beginning.

associations.name: Laurel Wreath
associations.significance: The laurel wreath symbolizes victory and achievement, representing the successful completion of the life journey.

associations.name: Circular Dance
associations.significance: A circular dance suggests the cyclical nature of existence and the continuous flow of energy and transformation.

associations.name: Indigo
associations.significance: Indigo represents deep intuition and spiritual understanding gained through experience and introspection.

associations.name: Four Corners
associations.significance: Four Corners signifies the integration of all aspects of life (mind, body, spirit, and emotions) into a unified whole.

associations.name: Sun
associations.significance: The sun represents enlightenment and clarity, illuminating the path forward after a period of darkness or confusion.

associations.name: Olive Branch
associations.significance: The olive branch is a symbol of peace and harmony, signifying the inner tranquility achieved through self-acceptance.

associations.name: Transcendence
associations.significance: Transcendence embodies the overcoming of limitations and the realization of one's full potential.

companions.name: Gaia
companions.description: As the embodiment of Earth, Gaia provides a foundation of stability and abundance for the World's completion, offering protection and grounding.

companions.name: Hermes
companions.description: Hermes, the messenger god, acts as a guide and facilitator, ensuring smooth transitions between cycles and delivering news of the World's achievements.

companions.name: Phoenix
companions.description: The Phoenix symbolizes rebirth and renewal, representing the cyclical nature of existence and the promise of new beginnings after completion.

companions.name: The Four Evangelists
companions.description: Represented by the bull, lion, eagle, and man, the Four Evangelists, or the four fixed signs of the zodiac, symbolize the stability and structure that supports the World's complete form.

companions.name: Ariadne
companions.description: Having guided Theseus out of the labyrinth, Ariadne represents guidance through complex journeys, aiding in navigating the final stages of the World's cycle.
